intro: Cotylelobium harmandii, also known as the Cotylelobium tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Fabaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The tree has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with its health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cotylelobium harmandii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cotylelobium harmandii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as to support the treatment of fever and coughs.
